description_long: This shrub consists of three slender, upright stems with elongated, narrow inflorescences at their tips. The stems are thin and greenish-brown, supporting clusters of small, tightly packed spikelets that give the tops a feathery, textured appearance. The spikelets are a muted brown with hints of tan, suggesting a grass-like or wild meadow species. Sparse, small leaves or bracts are visible along the lower portions of the stems, but the upper sections are dominated by the seed heads. The overall form is airy and delicate, with a natural, slightly irregular arrangement. This type of shrub is commonly found in open, grassy areas or as ornamental planting in urban parks and roadside verges.
description: A delicate, grass-like shrub with three slender stems topped by feathery brown seed heads and sparse lower leaves. The form is airy and natural, typical of wild grasses or ornamental urban plantings.
description_view_0: Three upright stems with feathery brown seed heads are clearly visible, showing the full width and density of the shrub.
description_view_1: The stems appear more closely aligned, with one stem mostly edge-on, emphasizing the thinness and verticality of the shrub.
description_view_2: A nearly frontal view, similar to view 0, but with a slight rotation revealing more of the rightmost stem's seed head.
description_view_3: The stems are more separated, with the leftmost stem appearing thinner and the seed heads showing a more side-on profile.
